VINIT.justification: The initial state before any conjunctive requirements are imposed.

TANCH.justification: The terminal definition of the conjunctive interaction mode within the grammar.

AFWD.justification: The forward movement of adding a new condition to the conjunctive set.

AREV.justification: The removal or descent from a specific requirement back toward a broader state.

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of multiple conditions into a single conjunctive requirement.

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity operation where a requirement is verified against itself.

FSPLIT.justification: The branching of a single state into multiple conditions that must all be satisfied.

FFUSE.justification: The reconstitution of the original state only if all parallel conditions are met.

EVALT.justification: The state where a condition is successfully met.

EVALF.justification: The state where a condition fails to be met.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where a condition is both required and not yet determined.

IFIX.justification: The permanent recording of the conjunctive interaction in the crystal.
